fields.label: PDF generator
fields.rationale: wkhtmltopdf is the Frappe default and the most-tested path; reliable for classic Jinja print formats. chrome uses headless Chromium and renders modern CSS (flexbox / grid / web fonts) better, but requires a heavier runtime dependency and is measurably slower per render. Switch to chrome only when a specific print format needs modern CSS.

fields.label: Include letterhead by default
fields.rationale: Controls whether Print Formats wrap in the default Letter Head automatically. Default 1. Individual Print Formats can override via their own letter_head field.

fields.label: Default print style
fields.rationale: Optional. Names a Print Style (a CSS bundle) applied site-wide. Empty uses Frappe's default style.

fields.label: Send print as PDF attachment
fields.rationale: When 1 (default), print-sharing actions (email with print) attach a PDF rather than inlining the HTML. Rendering the PDF is slower; keep on unless the use case is purely HTML email.

fields.label: Allow printing draft documents
fields.rationale: Default 1. When 0, draft (docstatus=0) documents cannot be printed - enforces 'only submitted docs print' discipline for audit-sensitive workflows.

fields.label: Allow printing cancelled documents
fields.rationale: Default 0. Cancelled (docstatus=2) docs usually shouldn't print to avoid contractual confusion. Set 1 when audit trails require printed cancelled copies.

fields.label: Repeat letterhead header/footer on every page
fields.rationale: When 1 (default), the Letter Head header and footer repeat on every PDF page. When 0, they appear only on the first and last pages respectively. 0 produces cleaner multi-page reports.

fields.label: Enable raw printing (network printers)
fields.rationale: When 1, enables raw-command print formats (ESC/POS, ZPL) via a configured print_server. Default 0. Enable only if your deployment has a network print server listening.

fields.label: Print server URL (when raw_printing=1)
fields.rationale: Required when raw_printing=1. URL of the raw-print forwarding server. Empty disables raw printing regardless of the raw_printing flag.

fields.label: Print server auth (when print_server is set)
fields.rationale: Optional credentials for the raw-print server. Empty means no auth. Format depends on the server implementation (Basic, Bearer, etc.).
